title: Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate
authors: Dzmitry Bahdanau, Kyunghyun Cho, Yoshua Bengio
hook: This paper lets translation models focus on the right words at each step.
promise: You will understand how attention helps neural networks translate sentences more accurately by aligning source and target words dynamically.
prerequisite_note: You only need an intuition for how machines might turn one sentence into another, step by step.

glossary.plain: A two-part neural network where one half reads the input and the other generates the output.
A single summary of a whole sentence, often too compressed to remember every detail.
A way for the model to focus on different input words when generating each output word.
The process of linking a word in the output to relevant words in the input.
A method where the model weighs all input words, rather than picking just one at a time.
A system that uses neural networks to convert sentences from one language to another.

cards.title: Translation that pays attention
body: Learn how neural networks became much better at translation by learning to focus on the right words.

cards.title: How can a model know what matters?
body: Imagine translating a sentence word by word, but not always in the same order. Some words need to connect across the sentence.
Early neural translators squeezed the whole input into a single memory. What if the model could look back at the words it needs, just in time?

cards.title: From sentence to translation: aligning on the fly
body: First, the model reads the entire source sentence and encodes it into a series of vectors, one for each word.
Then, as it generates each output word, it calculates which source words are most relevant, assigning attention weights.
Finally, it produces the translation word by word, guided by these dynamic alignments, instead of relying on a single summary vector.
cards.middle: Calculate attention for each output step
cards.right: Generate translated word using focused context
cards.caption: Attention lets the model align each output word to the right input words dynamically.

cards.title: What happens if you squeeze too much?
body: Slide to compare translations using a single memory vs dynamic attention.
cards.offNote: The model forgets details, especially for long or complex sentences.
cards.onNote: The model can focus on relevant words, improving accuracy and fluency.

cards.title: Let the model look back
body: Instead of forcing the model to remember everything in one vector, Bahdanau and colleagues let it search for relevant words each time it generates a new word.
This soft attention allows the model to learn alignments between input and output words, making translations more accurate, especially for longer sentences.

cards.title: How much attention is enough?
body: Move the slider to see how focusing too little or too much affects translation quality.
cards.low: The model ignores input context, producing poor translations.
cards.mid: The model balances context, improving accuracy and fluency.
cards.high: The model may overfit to input, sometimes copying instead of translating.

cards.title: What does this translation engine need?
body: Pick the ingredients that let a neural network align and translate sentences with attention.

choices.label: Word embeddings
choices.note: Represent words as vectors the model can process.

choices.label: Encoder RNN
choices.note: Reads and processes the input sentence.

choices.label: Attention layer
choices.note: Calculates which input words to focus on at each step.

choices.label: Decoder RNN
choices.note: Generates the output sentence word by word.

choices.label: Alignment scores
choices.note: Measure how relevant each input word is for the current output word.

choices.label: Softmax function
choices.note: Turns alignment scores into attention weights.

cards.title: Translation with attention: the impact
body: On English to French translation, the attention model matched or beat the strongest systems of the time, especially on longer sentences.
The model's learned alignments agreed well with human intuition, showing it really did learn to focus on the right words.
cards.caption: Attention especially improves translation for longer, more complex sentences.

cards.title: Why attention changed everything
body: This paper showed that forcing models to use a single memory is limiting, especially for complex language tasks like translation.
By letting the model align and focus as needed, attention paved the way for modern breakthroughs in language understanding and generation.

recap.label: Encode
recap.note: Read the input sentence into word vectors.

recap.label: Attend
recap.note: Compute relevance of each input word for each output step.

recap.label: Align
recap.note: Use attention weights to find the right context.

recap.label: Translate
recap.note: Generate each output word using focused information.

cards.title: Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate
body: Read the original paper to see how attention mechanisms transformed machine translation.
